overview: NIDHI Prayas is a sub-scheme under the NIDHI Programme of the Department of Science and Technology (DST) that provides financial support and mentorship to innovators for converting their ideas into prototypes. It aims to support early-stage startups and innovators in developing proof-of-concept and prototype development through grant-based assistance. The scheme focuses on promoting innovation and entrepreneurship by offering funding, mentorship, and access to incubation facilities.
objectives: To enable translation of innovative ideas to prototypes
To provide a platform for faster experimentation and modify approaches in the journey to market
To generate innovative solutions relevant to local and global problems
To attract youth to undertake problem solving and product development
To enhance the pipeline in terms of quality and quantity of innovative startups
To build a vibrant innovation ecosystem by connecting innovators, academia, mentors and incubators
eligibility: Individual innovators, startups, and student innovators from academic institutions are eligible to apply. The applicant must be an Indian citizen and the innovation must be at the idea or prototype stage. The innovation should have potential for commercialization and social impact. Applicants must be associated with a recognized NIDHI Prayas Centre (incubator) for implementation.
benefits: Financial support for prototype development, access to mentorship and technical guidance, use of fabrication labs and infrastructure at NIDHI Prayas Centres, networking opportunities with investors and industry experts, and support for IP filing and product development.
financial_support: NIDHI Prayas provides grant-in-aid support of up to INR 10 lakhs per innovator or startup for prototype development. The funds are disbursed in milestones based on the progress of the project, subject to review and approval by the NIDHI Prayas Centre. The grant is non-repayable and does not require equity dilution.
application_process: 1. Identify and apply to a recognized NIDHI Prayas Centre (incubator) 2. Submit the application with details of the innovation, team, and development plan 3. Present the idea to the selection committee at the Centre 4. If selected, sign an agreement with the Centre 5. Receive the first tranche of funds upon approval 6. Develop the prototype as per the agreed milestones 7. Submit progress reports and utilization certificates 8. Receive subsequent tranches based on milestone completion 9. Final submission of prototype and project report 10. Receive final payment upon successful completion
required_documents: Detailed project proposal including innovation description, objectives, and methodology
Any prior work or IP related to the innovation
Letter of support from academic institution (if applicable)
Declaration of originality and ownership of the idea
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year based on the availability of funds and capacity at NIDHI Prayas Centres.
implementing_agency: Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India
caveats: Funds are disbursed only through recognized NIDHI Prayas Centres (incubators)
The grant must be used solely for prototype development as per the approved plan
Beneficiaries must submit periodic progress reports and financial statements
The innovation must not infringe on any existing intellectual property
Funding is subject to availability and approval by the NIDHI Prayas Centre
